basics.name: Rafał Leszko
basics.label: Lead AI Engineer (Real-time Generative AI)
basics.summary: Lead AI Engineer at Daydream/Livepeer and the lead developer on Scope — a real-time engine for interactive audio/video AI generation, running pipelines like video diffusion, LTX-2, and ACEStep on NVIDIA RTX 4090/5090 (and H100/H200). Working across the stack on real-time generative AI systems: architecture, rapid prototyping, and high- and low-level development. Prior experience at Google, CERN, and Hazelcast. Speaker at 70+ conferences including KubeCon (twice). Author of 'Continuous Delivery with Docker and Jenkins'.

work.name: Daydream / Livepeer
work.summary: Lead developer on Scope, a real-time engine for interactive audio/video AI generation. Own architecture, integrate new model research, and drive end-to-end performance. Scope runs pipelines like video diffusion, LTX-2, and ACEStep on NVIDIA RTX 4090/5090 (and H100/H200). Earlier at Livepeer: shipped core video infrastructure across Livepeer Node, Catalyst (decentralized media server), and Livepeer Studio. Progression: Go/Blockchain Engineer → Staff Engineer → Lead AI Engineer.
highlights: Lead developer on Scope — real-time engine for interactive audio/video AI generation
Integrate new model research: video diffusion, LTX-2, ACEStep
Drive end-to-end performance on NVIDIA RTX 4090/5090 and H100/H200
Shipped core video infrastructure across Livepeer Node, Catalyst, Livepeer Studio

work.name: Hazelcast
work.position: Cloud-Native Tech Lead (previously Cloud Software Engineer)
work.summary: Built Hazelcast's cloud integrations: the Kubernetes Operator, Helm Chart, plugins (Kubernetes, AWS, GCP, Azure, Zookeeper), and Docker images. Promoted to Cloud-Native Tech Lead in 2020.
highlights: Cloud plugins for Kubernetes, AWS, GCP, Azure, Zookeeper

work.name: Google
work.summary: Designed and built a microservice-based project-tracking system.
